headlines.text: Kevin Warsh sworn in as Fed Chair at White House, replacing Jerome Powell amid Iran war inflation surge
House Republicans cancel Iran war powers vote at the last minute, fearing they would lose
The Mandalorian and Grogu opens in theaters today, earns $12M in previews ahead of Memorial Day weekend
Stephen Colbert's Late Show airs its final episode, replaced by Byron Allen's Comics Unleashed starting tonight
Dow Jones hits new all-time high above 50,700 as Gulf states push for Iran peace deal
Memorial Day weekend gas prices hit 4-year highs at $4.55 per gallon nationally
Secretary of State Rubio says Iran nuclear negotiations show 'a little bit of movement'
Senate Republicans bolt to Memorial Day recess without voting on $72B ICE funding bill
Trump says he was 'an hour away' from ordering new Iran strikes but held off at Gulf allies' request
Colorado Democrats censure Governor Polis for commuting election denier Tina Peters' prison sentence

breaking.text: Kevin Warsh sworn in as Federal Reserve Chair at White House ceremony - first such swearing-in at the White House since Alan Greenspan in 1987

stories.hed: House GOP Pulls Iran War Powers Vote After Running Out of Excuses (and Votes)
stories.dek: Republicans were on the verge of losing a Democratic-led war powers resolution that would have forced Trump to end the Iran war, so they did what any confident majority does: they canceled the vote entirely and went on vacation. Congress won't revisit this until June 1, meaning Trump retains full bomb-whenever-he-wants energy through Memorial Day.

stories.hed: Israel Deports 400+ Gaza Flotilla Activists After Seizing Them in International Waters
stories.dek: More than 400 members of the Global Sumud Flotilla were flown from southern Israel to Istanbul on chartered flights after being abducted in international waters. Activists arrived describing physical and psychological abuse, which is not exactly the five-star cruise experience they signed up for.

stories.hed: Kevin Warsh Takes Over the Fed at the Worst Possible Moment
stories.dek: Trump's hand-picked Federal Reserve chair was sworn in Friday at a White House ceremony featuring Clarence Thomas and approximately all the pressure in the world. Warsh inherits inflation at a 3-year high, mortgage rates surging, and a boss who already publicly fantasizes about suing him if he doesn't cut rates fast enough.

stories.hed: Trump's $1.8B DOJ 'Slush Fund' for MAGA Allies Turns Senate Hearing Into a Screaming Match
stories.dek: Senate Republicans met with AG Todd Blanche behind closed doors to demand limits on who could cash in on the new DOJ settlement fund, and the meeting reportedly became a screaming-fest when Blanche refused to narrow the criteria. George Santos and Rod Blagojevich have already publicly announced they want a slice.

stories.hed: CBS Replaces Stephen Colbert With Byron Allen's 'Comics Unleashed' Starting Tonight, and Yes That Is Real
stories.dek: The Late Show with Stephen Colbert aired its final episode on May 21 after 11 seasons, and CBS immediately handed the slot to a non-political syndicated comedy show owned by media mogul Byron Allen, who also just bought BuzzFeed. Trump critics call it regulatory capture; CBS calls it a financial decision. Both can be true.

markets.context: Dow hit a fresh all-time high above 50,700 as Gulf states UAE, Saudi Arabia, and Qatar pressured Trump to give Iran peace negotiations a chance, lifting risk sentiment; oil also rose on continued Strait of Hormuz supply concerns.

gas.note: Filling up for Memorial Day weekend now costs more than it did at the peak of 2022, which is somehow worse than it sounds.

popCulture.hed: The Mandalorian and Grogu Brings Star Wars Back to Theaters After 7 Years, Opens to $12M in Thursday Previews
popCulture.dek: Pedro Pascal and Baby Yoda make the leap from Disney+ to the big screen today, projecting a $80-100M Memorial Day weekend haul. Critics gave it a 63% on Rotten Tomatoes while audiences gave it an 89%, which is the most Star Wars thing that could possibly happen.

genZ.term: Devil Wears Prada 'and Emily... that's all' TikTok trend
genZ.definition: Triggered by the release of Devil Wears Prada 2 earlier this month, TikTok users are flooding the platform with the 'and Emily... that's all' audio format, contrasting two things where one is polished and correct and the other is an absolute disaster. It has become one of the most used audio formats of May 2026 with millions of videos across niches.
genZ.usage: House Republicans scheduling then quietly canceling the Iran war powers vote: 'the war powers resolution... and the Memorial Day recess... that's all.'

tldr: Kevin Warsh was sworn in as Fed Chair at the White House today as the Dow hit a new all-time high, while House Republicans fled to their Memorial Day vacations having quietly killed a vote to rein in Trump's Iran war — a war now in its 84th day with peace talks described as having 'a little bit of movement,' which is doing a lot of heavy lifting. The Mandalorian and Baby Yoda opened in theaters and Stephen Colbert said goodbye to late night, leaving America to process both developments over $4.55-a-gallon gasoline this weekend.
